plan.title: Plan: Personalized LinkedIn-backed review links for clean.dev
plan.overview:
This feature adds a new public review/reference flow to the existing `apps/web` Next.js application so you can send personalized deep links to former colleagues and let them submit a testimonial on `clean.dev`. The core design should avoid storing review data in the application database: each invite link will carry a signed, time-bounded token, the review form will submit directly to a delivery channel such as email, and LinkedIn OAuth will be used only to prefill identity details and optionally link back to the reviewer’s public profile.

Because this is a **Production Ready** feature for external users, the plan includes more than just the happy path. It covers signed-link authorization, LinkedIn OAuth integration in `apps/web/auth.ts`, graceful failure when LinkedIn is unavailable, structured observability, rate limiting and abuse controls, integration and load validation, deployment behind a feature flag, and a rollback/runbook path. No new persistent application state or database migration is planned, which narrows scope and keeps rollback simple.

A key architectural decision is to treat the personalized deep link itself as the authorization boundary instead of introducing a new review database or admin CRUD UI. That keeps delivery fast within the stated timeline, but it means invite generation, token signing, token expiry, and replay/abuse controls must be implemented carefully in modules such as `apps/web/src/lib/review-links.ts` and `apps/web/src/app/reviews/actions.ts`. LinkedIn login should be optional-but-prominent: reviewers must still be able to complete the flow if OAuth fails, while signed invites prevent anonymous public abuse.

Scope boundaries: this plan does **not** include a new dashboard for managing reviews, long-term storage of review submissions, or a CMS workflow for publishing testimonials. The deliverable is a secure production feature that accepts invited review submissions on the web, enriches them with LinkedIn identity when available, forwards them to your existing inbound channel, and gives operators clear deployment, monitoring, and rollback procedures.
